entries.title: Browser Request Annotation Sticky
entries.notes: Builds a side panel overlay on Network tab allowing text annotations per request. Ships as a DevTools extension snippet or full manifest. Now matters because local-first storage + sync makes cross-session debugging sticky. No competitor lets you annotate network waterfall entries in-place.
entries.pain: Devs lose context when reviewing network requests later because they cannot add notes to specific API calls in DevTools.

entries.title: Gmail Attachment Open Receipt Detector
entries.notes: Injects a tracking pixel into PDF/Excel attachments on send and shows a toast notification when opened. Lightweight fetch endpoint needed. Now matters because attachment-based outreach is high-stakes and timing-sensitive. Gmail lacks native open receipts for attachments.
entries.pain: Sales reps sending proposals via Gmail do not know when recipients actually open attachments, losing timing advantage on follow-ups.

entries.title: CSS Scroll-Driven Animation Inspector
entries.notes: Parses CSS scroll-driven animation specs and renders a scrubber showing progress at each scroll position. Uses Intersection Observer + scroll events. Now matters because scroll-driven animations shipped in 2024 and tooling lags. No extension focuses on this specific debugging angle.
entries.pain: Frontend devs struggle to debug scroll-driven animations because Chrome DevTools lacks a dedicated panel for visualizing timeline progress.

entries.title: LinkedIn Recruiter Inbox Duplicate Detector
entries.notes: Scrapes LinkedIn recruiter messages, fingerprints job titles and companies, clusters duplicates, and shows a badge. Requires careful LinkedIn ToS handling. Now matters because agency recruiting fragmentation makes this common. No tool focuses on recruiter message deduplication.
entries.pain: Job seekers receive the same role from multiple recruiters but cannot easily identify duplicates to avoid negotiating against themselves.

entries.title: GCP Credits Expiration Sentinel
entries.notes: Queries GCP billing API on interval and shows a persistent badge when credits are within 7 days of expiring. Configurable threshold. Now matters because many indie devs and startups rely on trial credits and forget. No Chrome extension surfaces this directly.
entries.pain: Developers on GCP free tier or trial credits lose services unexpectedly because expiration dates are buried in billing dashboards.

entries.title: Browser Clipboard Semantic Search
entries.notes: Stores clipboard entries locally with embeddings for semantic search. Side panel UI with fuzzy match. Now matters because AI tools generate more clipboard-worthy snippets. No browser-native history supports semantic search.
entries.pain: Users with long clipboard histories cannot find past copied items because they only remember the content, not the timestamp.

entries.title: JSON Response Diff on Repeat Requests
entries.notes: Detects duplicate request patterns in Network tab, stores previous payloads, and shows inline diff in Response panel. Lightweight diff algorithm. Now matters because API stability testing is manual and tedious. No DevTools extension does auto-diff on repeated requests.
entries.pain: QA engineers manually compare API responses across identical requests because no tool auto-highlights what changed between calls.

entries.title: PDF Reading Time Estimator
entries.notes: Extracts text from in-browser PDF via pdf.js, counts words, applies WPM estimate, shows a badge. Now matters because PDF consumption in-browser is increasing. No extension gives instant reading-time estimates for PDFs.
entries.pain: Professionals opening research papers or contracts in-browser have no quick sense of how long they will spend reading before committing.

entries.title: Email Warmup Config Deliverability Tester
entries.notes: Sends test emails through configured warmup pools to known seed accounts and reports deliverability signals. Now matters because warmup services proliferate but verification is absent. No tool lets you test warmup effectiveness on-demand.
entries.pain: Sales teams using email warmup tools do not know if their warmup configuration is actually improving inbox placement.

entries.title: Webhook Schema Example Generator
entries.notes: Detects webhook endpoints in Network tab, infers schema from payloads, generates TypeScript/fake-data examples in side panel. Now matters because webhook-first integrations are growing. No extension auto-generates test payloads from live webhook captures.
entries.pain: Backend developers building webhook integrations waste time constructing realistic payloads because documentation lacks working examples.

entries.title: GitHub PR Story Point Estimator
entries.notes: Reads diff size, file count, test coverage, and review history from GitHub PR page, applies a heuristic model, suggests a point value. Now matters because PR-based estimation workflows are common but tooling is clunky. No extension brings this to the PR page directly.
entries.pain: Engineering managers cannot quickly estimate story points for PRs because no tool analyzes PR characteristics to suggest point values.

entries.title: Browser Network Retry Count Overlay
entries.notes: Instruments fetch/XHR to track retry count, shows a badge per request in Network tab. Now matters because retry-heavy APIs are common and debugging retries is painful. No DevTools extension surfaces retry count as a first-class metric.
entries.pain: Frontend developers cannot see how many times each network request retried without digging into individual request timing details.

entries.title: Gmail Unsubscribe Shortcut Injector
entries.notes: Detects List-Unsubscribe headers and injects a prominent one-click unsubscribe button in Gmail. Now matters because CCPA/GDPR rights exist but exercising them is tedious. No extension auto-surfaces List-Unsubscribe links in Gmail's UI.
entries.pain: Users frustrated by email newsletters have no quick way to unsubscribe because the link is buried behind multiple clicks.

entries.title: Browser DOM Mutation Diff Replayer
entries.notes: Records MutationObserver events and generates a reproducible script to replay mutations. Side panel shows a timeline of DOM changes. Now matters because SPAs have more complex DOM dynamics. No extension combines recording + replay with export capability.
entries.pain: Debugging intermittent DOM bugs is hard because developers cannot replay exactly what DOM mutations occurred between two points in time.
